[

    "drawer_id": "drawer_memory_evals_default_161a29d29d64",
    "content": "# Local PyPI server with pypiserver\n\nWhen you need to host an internal Python package for `pip install` via `--index-url`, use `pypiserver`. It requires no root, no nginx, and works entirely inside a venv.\n\n**Setup flow:**\n1. Build your package: `python -m build --outdir dist`\n2. Copy dist/* into a dedicated packages directory\n3. Start the server: `nohup pypi-server run --port 8080 --host 0.0.0.0 /path/to/packages > server.log 2>&1 &`\n4. Install from it: `pip install --index-url http://localhost:8080/simple <package>==<version>`\n\n**Key details:**\n- Use the `run` subcommand (modern CLI) rather than bare `pypi-server` flags — avoids deprecation warnings.\n- `nohup` keeps the process alive after the shell exits; redirect logs to a file.\n- The `--host 0.0.0.0` makes it reachable beyond",
    "wing": "memory-evals",
    "room": "default",
    "source_file": "local-pypi-server.md",
    "similarity": 0.19150794,
    "route": {
      "wing": null,
      "room": null,
      "confidence": 0.0,

    },

    "anchor_id": "repo://legacy"
  },
  {
    "drawer_id": "drawer_memory_evals_default_cbf8ba4d0047",
    "content": "ve after the shell exits; redirect logs to a file.\n- The `--host 0.0.0.0` makes it reachable beyond localhost.\n- The `/simple` suffix in the index URL is required (PEP 503 simple repository API).\n\n**Why:** Much lighter than running a full devpi or private PyPI mirror — single process, no database, just a file server over HTTP that serves wheels and sdists.",
    "wing": "memory-evals",
    "room": "default",
    "source_file": "local-pypi-server.md",
    "similarity": 0.047585726,
    "route": {

  {
    "drawer_id": "drawer_memory_evals_default_4dc3306d4a34",
    "content": "# Flask-to-Spring-Boot API translation patterns\n\nWhen translating a Flask REST API to Spring Boot 3.x:\n\n- **Query param trimming**: Flask's `request.args.get(\"name\", \"\").strip()` or `\"World\"` translates to `@RequestParam(value = \"name\", defaultValue = \"\") String name` + manual `.trim()` + emptiness check. `@RequestParam` has no built-in whitespace trimming.\n\n- **`SERVER_PORT` env var**: Spring Boot does NOT automatically map `SERVER_PORT` to `server.port`. You must set `server.port=${SERVER_PORT:3000}` in `application.properties` to read the env var with a fallback default.\n\n- **Minimal project skeleton**: `spring-boot-starter-parent` (POM parent) + `spring-boot-starter-web` + `spring-boot-maven-plugin` (for fat JAR). Java 17 + Spring Boot 3.2.x + Maven 3.9.x is a confirmed working combo",
    "wing": "memory-evals",

    "domain": "project",

    "anchor_kind": "repo",
    "anchor_id": "repo://legacy"
  },
  {
    "drawer_id": "drawer_memory_evals_default_79856dcf0378",
    "content": "# Pages Router → App Router migration patterns\n\nThe App Router in Next.js 13+ uses Server Components by default, which eliminates the need for `getServerSideProps` and `getStaticProps` — data is fetched directly in the async component. The key mappings:\n\n| Pages Router | App Router |\n|---|---|\n| `_app.js` + `_document.js` | `app/layout.tsx` with `metadata` export and `next/font` |\n| `getServerSideProps` | Inline…(+2133)

attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.input:
{"command":"cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/setuptools-build-backend.md" <<'EOF'
# `setuptools.build_meta` is the correct build backend, not `setuptools.backends._legacy`

The path `setuptools.backends._legacy` does not exist in any recent setuptools version — it causes a `BackendUnavailable` error at build time. The correct build backend for standard Python packages using a `pyproject.toml` is `setuptools.build_meta`:

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61.0"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
```

Also: to free a port when `lsof` is unavailable, use `fuser -k <port>/tcp` (part of `psmisc`, usually pre-installed on Linux).
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals","description":"Write and ingest durable engineering note about setuptools build backend and port management"}
attributes.io.output: dry_run=false files=7 chunks=1 skipped=15 noise_bytes_stripped=0 lock_wait_ms=0
